title: shirakiopsis elliptica
intro: Shirakiopsis elliptica, also known as the East Indian rosewood, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Shirakiopsis elliptica include treating fever, pain, and skin conditions, as well as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Shirakiopsis elliptica include extracts, oils, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
